title: Veloci-Tea
logline: A genetically engineered Velociraptor escapes a shady biotech lab and hides in a failing Portland tea shop, where her refined nose for perfect blends gives a sarcastic widow one last reason to keep brewing.
tone: Heartwarming, odd-couple, whimsical indie comedy; Amelie meets Paddington, never scary.
era: Contemporary rainy Portland with cozy artisanal tea culture and lightly absurd biotech noir.

characters.name: Mara Bell
characters.description: Sarcastic, burnt-out widow in her early 40s who owns the failing artisanal Portland tea shop Steep Endings; grieving, dry-witted, guarded but kind.
characters.wardrobe: Rumpled natural-linen apron over dark practical layers, worn boots, loose sleeves, tea towel always nearby.

profile.physical_signature: Early 40s, tired posture softening over the pilot, expressive skeptical eyebrow, ink-stained hands from menu chalk.
profile.face: Warm but exhausted face with fine smile lines and guarded eyes.
profile.hair: Messy dark-brown bob usually tucked behind one ear.
profile.body: Average height, slightly hunched from stress, relaxed by the finale.
profile.movement: Efficient, clipped motions that become gentler around Oolong.
profile.personality: Sarcastic, grieving, practical, secretly tender, protective.
profile.consistency_notes: Keep her visually grounded and human; her arc is from isolation to cautious delight.
profile.negative_prompt: No glam makeover, no cruelty, no action-hero styling.

characters.name: Oolong
characters.description: A 7-foot-tall genetically engineered feathered Velociraptor whose refined sense of smell makes her a Michelin-level tea sommelier; gentle, bashful, expressive, and non-scary.
characters.wardrobe: No clothing except occasional accidental tea sack scarf or dusting of matcha; never armored or monstrous.

profile.physical_signature: Tall feathered Velociraptor with iridescent moss-green and chestnut plumage, large curious amber eyes, nimble claws, long balancing tail, friendly posture.
profile.face: Expressive reptilian face softened by feathers and wide amber eyes, capable of bashful comedy.
profile.hair: Feather crest rather than hair, slightly ruffled when nervous.
profile.eyes: Large amber eyes, intelligent, gentle, apologetic.
profile.body: Seven feet tall, athletic but careful, feathered, never horror-coded.
profile.movement: Precise claw gestures, delicate tiptoeing, silent-film comedic timing, tea-sommelier focus when sniffing.
profile.personality: Curious, gentle, tea-obsessed, shy, loyal, proud of perfect blends.
profile.voice: Soft chirps, purrs, and tiny pleased trills; no roars.
profile.consistency_notes: Always heartwarming and non-threatening; show tea genius through smell, steam, color, and exact claw pinches.
profile.negative_prompt: No blood, no snarling predator shots, no horror lighting, no attacking customers.

characters.name: Devon Pike
characters.description: A nervous biotech handler trying to retrieve Oolong without causing a scene; fussy, corporate, and increasingly undone by tea-shop etiquette.
characters.wardrobe: Too-clean gray windbreaker, laminated badge, rain-damp trousers, sensible shoes, clipboard or missing-asset flyer.

profile.physical_signature: Nervous posture, tidy hair flattened by rain, corporate badge always slightly crooked.
profile.movement: Fussy small steps, clipboard clutching, awkward peering through windows.
profile.personality: Rule-bound, anxious, polite, baffled by warmth.
profile.voice: Careful corporate politeness cracking into confusion.
profile.consistency_notes: Antagonistic pressure without menace; more bureaucratic nuisance than villain.

characters.name: June Alvarez
characters.description: A loyal neighborhood regular in her 60s with bright glasses, blunt warmth, and a love of gossip; first customer astonished by Oolong's blend.
characters.wardrobe: Colorful raincoat, bright glasses, patterned scarf, practical Portland shoes.

profile.physical_signature: Bright glasses, warm lined face, expressive hands wrapped around mugs.
profile.movement: Direct, cheerful, nosy, comfortable in the shop.
profile.consistency_notes: Her delighted reaction sells the secret blend to the neighborhood.

locations.name: Steep Endings Tea Shop
locations.description: A failing cozy artisanal tea shop in rainy Portland with mismatched chairs, hand-lettered chalkboard menus, shelves of tea tins, hanging plants, foggy windows, and a cramped back room full of sacks, kettles, scales, and shipping boxes.

locations.name: Biotech Alley
locations.description: A damp service alley behind a shady biotech lab, blue security lights, steam vents, loading doors, wet pavement, and absurdly polite warning signs.

locations.name: Portland Sidewalk
locations.description: Rain-slick sidewalk outside Steep Endings with bicycles, umbrellas, food-cart lights, indie posters, passing curious neighbors, and warm shop light spilling onto puddles.

do: Keep Oolong gentle, bashful, expressive, and comedic rather than frightening.
Use cozy warm practical tea-shop light against cool Portland rain.
Show tea blending through visible steam, scent swirls, precise claw gestures, and tactile ingredients.
Maintain whimsical indie comedy framing with soft storybook charm.
dont: Do not make Oolong scary, bloody, roaring, or predatory.
Do not stage action violence or horror suspense.
Do not contradict character locks in shot prompts.
Do not make the shop sleek or successful before the neighborhood discovers the blends.

reference_notes: 5-minute pilot movie, 300 seconds total, planned as 30 shots at 10 seconds each. Whimsical indie comedy with heartwarming odd-couple energy.
